name: Sundel Bolong
description: The Sundel Bolong is a ghostly figure originating from Indonesian folklore, often depicted as a beautiful woman with long black hair and a distinctive hole in her back. This hole is said to be where her baby was aborted or stillborn, and it is often concealed by her hair or clothing. The Sundel Bolong is believed to be a vengeful spirit, seeking to avenge her tragic fate and the loss of her child. She is said to lure men with her beauty and charm, only to reveal her gruesome back and inflict harm or misfortune upon them. Stories of the Sundel Bolong serve as cautionary tales, often warning against premarital sex, abortion, and the abandonment of children. Her presence is often associated with a strong, sweet fragrance, similar to that of jasmine or frangipani flowers, which can both attract and repel those who encounter her. The origins of the Sundel Bolong vary depending on the region and the specific tale, but the common thread is the tragic loss of a child and the spirit's subsequent desire for retribution. She is a symbol of societal anxieties surrounding female sexuality, motherhood, and the consequences of violating social norms. Encounters with her are considered highly dangerous, and various rituals and charms are believed to offer protection against her wrath. The Sundel Bolong remains a prominent figure in Indonesian popular culture, appearing in films, television shows, and literature, continuing to terrify and fascinate audiences.
subtitle: The Ghost with a Hole
appearance: A beautiful woman with long black hair, pale skin, and a large, gaping hole in her back, often hidden by her hair or clothing. She typically wears a long white dress.
habitat: Roads, graveyards, abandoned buildings, and areas near villages or towns, particularly at night.
behavior: The Sundel Bolong lures men with her beauty and charm, often appearing as a lost or distressed woman. Once she has gained their trust, she reveals the hole in her back and inflicts harm or misfortune upon them. She is also said to weep inconsolably, mourning the loss of her child.

keyMyths.title: The Origin of the Hole
keyMyths.description: The hole in the Sundel Bolong's back is believed to be the result of a forced abortion or a stillbirth, often caused by societal pressures or unwanted pregnancies. This tragic event is the source of her vengeful spirit and her desire to seek retribution on men.

keyMyths.title: Luring Men to Their Doom
keyMyths.description: Sundel Bolong is known for her ability to lure men with her beauty and charm, often appearing as a damsel in distress. Once she has gained their trust, she reveals her gruesome back, causing them shock, fear, and ultimately, misfortune or even death.

sources.author: Various Authors
sources.description: Compilations of stories and myths passed down through generations.
sources.text: Compilations of stories and myths passed down through generations.

sources.author: Indonesian Film Industry
sources.description: Various films that have depicted the Sundel Bolong, contributing to her popular image.
sources.text: Various films that have depicted the Sundel Bolong, contributing to her popular image.

variants: ghost with a hole in her back
